title: Print PDF pipeline: Pandoc-based conversion from docs/_chapters/ markdown into KDP-ready paperback PDF (6x9 trim, correct margins/bleed/gutter, running heads, page numbers, clean chapter breaks). Typography tuned to match the web aesthetic (Lora body + Playfair Display chapter headings, sized for print). Output lands at print/out/wargame-design-book.pdf.

Cover template: front + spine + back assembled as SVG (or Pandoc-driven), with spine-width calculator as a function of interior page count using KDP's paper-factor formula. Placeholders for title/subtitle/author/back-cover copy (filled by wdb-008). Reusable for future Ray KDP books.
interactive_only_reason: Cover artwork + aesthetic register are Ray-taste decisions. Bot was picking this every WDB cycle and exit-2-failing because the engineer can't make the aesthetic call. Flipped 2026-04-24 evening so bot stops retrying it. Spine-width calculator (the bot-doable subset) can graduate to its own task once Ray picks the cover aesthetic.

title: Front + back matter scaffolding: title page, copyright page with ISBN placeholder, auto-generated table of contents (derived from chapter front matter), about-the-author stub, also-by list, acknowledgements page. Copy content itself (author bio, back-cover hook) is wdb-008.

KDP proof-copy workflow doc: how to order a KDP paperback proof, what to check (trim, bleed, inside/outside margins, text legibility, image clarity at 300 DPI), iteration loop, known gotchas. Reusable across future Ray KDP projects.

KDP launch checklist: listing fields (title, subtitle, series, edition, description, author bio), category selection template, keyword-research worksheet, review-request email templates, D-day sequence (pricing, promotion, first-24h checks). Reusable across future KDP projects.

KDP royalty analysis: at $20 retail, KDP paperback 60% royalty minus printing cost (black-ink, 6x9 trim, page count). Break-even per copy, comparison table across $15/$18/$20/$25 retail price points, monthly income projection at N copies/month scenarios. Output drives wdb-010 pricing decision.

Amazon listing description: short (200-char hook) + long (4000-char body). Voice-calibrated against the book's own opening chapters (docs/_chapters/chapter_01.md, chapter_02.md). Stop-slop pass mandatory before landing — this is public-facing copy where AI-tells get read as AI-tells. SHIPPED 2026-04-26 evening as part of the cursor/wdb-voice-drafts merge to main; lives at print/copy/amazon-listing.md. Ray approved drafts via paste-review without changes.
interactive_only_reason: Public-facing Amazon listing copy, voice-sensitive. Ray writes with /stop-slop pass.

title: Author bio + back-cover hook copy. Bio for the book jacket + Amazon listing; hook is the pitch someone reads in a store aisle. Both voice-sensitive. Should reference Conflict Simulations LLC without turning the bio into a CSL ad. SHIPPED 2026-04-26 evening as part of the cursor/wdb-voice-drafts merge: print/copy/author-bio.md (short 150w + long 300w) + print/copy/back-cover-hook.md (Candidate 2 selected, candidates 1 and 3 preserved as alternates). Ray approved via paste-review and delegated final candidate pick to Claude.
interactive_only_reason: Author voice for the physical book jacket. Taste work.

title: Amazon keyword + BISAC category selection: 7 keywords + 2 BISAC categories. Requires judgment about discovery positioning (hobby-games taxonomy vs. military-history taxonomy vs. cross-listing). Ray's call on where the book is best found. SHIPPED 2026-04-26 evening as part of the cursor/wdb-voice-drafts merge: print/copy/keywords-worksheet.md. Top-7 recommendation: wargame design / board game design / tabletop game design / conflict simulation / designing wargames / hex and counter wargames / historical board games. Top-2 BISAC: GAMES & ACTIVITIES / Board Games (GAM001000) + DESIGN / Games (DES016000).

Final KDP retail price decision after wdb-006 royalty analysis is in hand. Target is $20; actual list could move based on print cost, paperback-market comparables, and desired royalty per copy. SHIPPED 2026-04-26 evening as part of the cursor/wdb-voice-drafts merge: print/copy/pricing-recommendation.md. Recommendation: launch at $24.99 (60% Amazon-direct royalty produces ~$9.67/copy after $5.32 print cost; $19.99 only ~$6.67/copy). Hold off on Expanded Distribution; 40% royalty rate gives up too much on this niche.
interactive_only_reason: Business decision downstream of royalty analysis (wdb-006).

title: Font polish: swap Georgia -> Lora body + Playfair Display chapter heads per wdb-001 original spec. Variable-TTF files (ofl/lora + ofl/playfairdisplay) already downloaded and user-font-registered 2026-04-23 but triggered  at PDF-write stage. Two-path fix: (a) download static-TTF versions (variable fonts + dvipdfmx is the incompatibility) or (b) swap xelatex -> lualatex (different PDF pipeline, handles variable fonts natively). Update metadata.yaml + header-includes titleformat defs once the engine works.

Cover text size pass: re-render the cover (build-cover.py) with all text large enough to be readable by older readers (boomers). Ray flagged 2026-04-26 evening that the wdb-002 cover (commit 98ce07e on main) had at least one text element that was too small. Audit subtitle, byline, back-cover hook (now finalized via wdb-008), spine text, and any tagline text. Bump font sizes where they read tight, and re-render print/cover/cover.svg + the print-ready PDF derivative. Re-check the print pipeline's wdb-002 cover output integrates the Candidate-2 back-cover-hook from print/copy/back-cover-hook.md.
interactive_only_reason: Visual + typographic taste decision: which text elements need to grow, by how much, without breaking the cover composition. Ray's eye on the rendered output before re-shipping the print PDF.
_done_note: Shipped 2026-04-28 evening on main via wdb-013-cover-text-sizing-pass merge. Three commits: c3a2538 (Cursor CLI size bump for boomer readability), f629a21 (Cursor IDE Auto pulled title text back from spine/front boundaries per Ray's 04-27 annotation pass), b8f8c51 (Claude shifted spine author baseline 1.530 to 1.800 to clear top gold rule). Back-cover-hook integration is wdb-002, not closed by this task.

title: Stop-slop sweep on book chapters — voice audit pass against the same kind of AI-tells Ray scrubbed from the public Jekyll site. First pass landed 2026-04-26 evening (chapter_17.md: 'a human operator' -> 'an operator'). Sweep the rest of docs/_chapters/*.md for similar tells: rule-of-three / tricolons in places they don't belong, em-dashes in chapter prose, 'human X' qualifiers where the X obviously implies a person, 'delve' / 'leverage' / 'robust' / 'comprehensive' / 'seamless' filler vocabulary, throat-clearing chapter openings. Compare against Ray's voice from chapter_01 / chapter_02. Apply per-tell fixes; commit each cluster as its own atomic commit so Ray can roll back if any fix overcorrected the voice.
interactive_only_reason: Voice-bearing chapter content; needs Ray's eye on each flagged tell since some 'AI cadence' is actually his voice. Stop-slop tooling can flag, Ray approves, fixer applies.
cancelled_note: Cancelled 2026-04-27 morning per Ray: every chapter was stop-slopped at write time during initial drafting, so a separate sweep pass is unnecessary. The chapter_17 fix (commit 98ce07e on cursor/wdb-voice-drafts) was a single instance Ray spotted in passing, not the leading edge of a broader sweep.
